On this page

Examples ​

Practical examples showing how to extend AI Image Renamer using its hooks and filters.

Add a Prefix to All Filenames ​

Add a consistent prefix (e.g., your brand name) to every renamed file.

php
add_filter( 'air_new_filename', function ( string $filename ): string {
    return 'mysite-' . $filename;
}, 10, 1 );

Result: golden-retriever.jpg → mysite-golden-retriever.jpg

Skip Renaming for Specific Users ​

Disable auto-rename for non-admin users.

php
add_filter( 'air_should_process_upload', function ( bool $should_process ): bool {
    if ( ! current_user_can( 'manage_options' ) ) {
        return false;
    }
    return $should_process;
} );

Use a Custom AI Prompt ​

Replace the default prompt with one tailored to your content.

php
add_filter( 'air_prompt', function ( string $prompt, int $max_keywords ): string {
    return sprintf(
        'Describe this product image in exactly %d keywords. '
        . 'Focus on the product type, color, and material. '
        . 'Only return the keywords, separated by commas.',
        $max_keywords
    );
}, 10, 2 );

Log All Renames to a Custom Table ​

Track every rename operation in a custom database table.

php
add_action( 'air_image_renamed', function (
    string $new_filename,
    string $original_name,
    string $description,
    array $file
): void {
    global $wpdb;

    $wpdb->insert(
        $wpdb->prefix . 'air_rename_log',
        [
            'original_name' => $original_name,
            'new_name'      => $new_filename,
            'description'   => $description,
            'user_id'       => get_current_user_id(),
            'created_at'    => current_time( 'mysql' ),
        ],
        [ '%s', '%s', '%s', '%d', '%s' ]
    );
}, 10, 4 );

Add WooCommerce Product SKU to Filenames ​

If you're running a WooCommerce store, prepend the product SKU to image filenames.

php
add_filter( 'air_new_filename', function ( string $filename ): string {
    // Check if this upload is happening in a product edit screen
    if ( ! isset( $_REQUEST['post_id'] ) ) {
        return $filename;
    }

    $post_id = absint( $_REQUEST['post_id'] );
    $product = wc_get_product( $post_id );

    if ( ! $product ) {
        return $filename;
    }

    $sku = $product->get_sku();
    if ( ! empty( $sku ) ) {
        return sanitize_title( $sku ) . '-' . $filename;
    }

    return $filename;
} );

Result: golden-retriever-toy.jpg → prod-12345-golden-retriever-toy.jpg

Increase the File Size Limit ​

Allow larger images to be processed (e.g., for high-resolution photography).

php
add_filter( 'air_max_file_size', function (): int {
    return 25 * 1024 * 1024; // 25 MB
} );

Override Twig Templates ​

Replace the plugin's admin templates with your own from a Pro add-on or custom plugin.

php
add_filter( 'air_template_paths', function ( array $paths ): array {
    // Prepend your custom views directory — it takes priority
    array_unshift( $paths, MY_PRO_PLUGIN_DIR . 'views' );
    return $paths;
} );

Now create a file like views/admin/settings.twig in your plugin and it will override the core template.

Add a Custom Model ​

Register a new AI model in the settings dropdown.

php
// Step 1: Add the model to the UI
add_filter( 'air_available_models', function ( array $models ): array {
    $models['my-provider/my-model'] = [
        'label'      => 'My Custom Model',
        'provider'   => 'my-provider',
        'desc'       => 'Optimized for e-commerce product images.',
        'highlights' => ['speed', 'params'],
        'specs'      => [
            'params'  => '13B',
            'context' => '64k',
            'speed'   => '500 tokens/s',
            'size'    => '15 MB',
        ],
    ];
    return $models;
} );

// Step 2: Allow the model ID to pass validation
add_filter( 'air_valid_models', function ( array $models ): array {
    $models[] = 'my-provider/my-model';
    return $models;
} );

Capitalize Alt Text as Full Sentences ​

Transform the generated alt text into a proper sentence.

php
add_filter( 'air_alt_text', function ( string $alt_text ): string {
    // "golden retriever playing fetch sunny park"
    // → "Golden retriever playing fetch sunny park."
    $alt_text = ucfirst( strtolower( $alt_text ) );

    if ( ! str_ends_with( $alt_text, '.' ) ) {
        $alt_text .= '.';
    }

    return $alt_text;
} );

Debug API Responses ​

Log API responses to help debug issues during development.

php
add_action( 'air_api_response', function ( ?array $decoded, int $code, string $image_path ): void {
    if ( ! defined( 'WP_DEBUG' ) || ! WP_DEBUG ) {
        return;
    }

    error_log( sprintf(
        'AIR Debug: API response for %s (HTTP %d): %s',
        basename( $image_path ),
        $code,
        wp_json_encode( $decoded )
    ) );
}, 10, 3 );

Add Custom Settings Fields ​

Extend the settings page with your own options.

php
// Register the field
add_action( 'air_register_settings_fields', function ( string $page_slug ): void {
    add_settings_field(
        'custom_prefix',
        'Filename Prefix',
        function () {
            $options = get_option( 'air_options', [] );
            $value   = $options['custom_prefix'] ?? '';
            printf(
                '<input type="text" name="air_options[custom_prefix]" value="%s" class="regular-text" />',
                esc_attr( $value )
            );
            echo '<p class="description">Prefix added to all renamed files.</p>';
        },
        $page_slug,
        'air_advanced_section'
    );
} );

// Sanitize the field
add_filter( 'air_sanitize_settings', function ( array $sanitized, array $input ): array {
    $sanitized['custom_prefix'] = sanitize_title( $input['custom_prefix'] ?? '' );
    return $sanitized;
}, 10, 2 );

// Set the default
add_filter( 'air_settings_defaults', function ( array $defaults ): array {
    $defaults['custom_prefix'] = '';
    return $defaults;
} );
